top_priority.scientific_question: Does higher local source inventory intensify release or blockage without changing drag?
top_priority.success_gate_for_critical_claim: target time reached; no atom loss; Bmax increases by at least an order of magnitude over loading scan or source fraction remains high with growing local inventory
top_priority.hydraulic_context: current loading cases remain in a pre-clogging hydraulic perturbation regime; stronger local release or higher retained debris loading is needed for critical blockage evidence
top_priority.boundary: candidate-planning evidence only; critical blockage requires target-time DEM plus blockage/connectivity/hydraulic metrics

evidence_boundary: Critical blockage is not yet proven; this matrix prioritizes target-time DEM evidence needed to test it.

records.success_gate_for_critical_claim: target time reached; no atom loss; Bmax increases by at least an order of magnitude over loading scan or source fraction remains high with growing local inventory

records.scientific_question: Does the upstream internal source eventually produce outlet breakthrough over the full 40 ms window?
records.success_gate_for_critical_claim: 40 ms reached; no atom loss; outlet fraction or x_max/L trend distinguishes delayed tail from permanent retention
records.hydraulic_context: current loading cases remain in a pre-clogging hydraulic perturbation regime; stronger local release or higher retained debris loading is needed for critical blockage evidence
records.boundary: candidate-planning evidence only; critical blockage requires target-time DEM plus blockage/connectivity/hydraulic metrics

records.scientific_question: Does a downstream source reach the outlet faster than the upstream source under the same physical drag?
records.success_gate_for_critical_claim: target time reached; source-position effect quantified; use as comparison, not critical transition alone
records.current_interpretation: leading tail reaches near outlet but outlet count remains zero
records.hydraulic_context: current loading cases remain in a pre-clogging hydraulic perturbation regime; stronger local release or higher retained debris loading is needed for critical blockage evidence
records.boundary: candidate-planning evidence only; critical blockage requires target-time DEM plus blockage/connectivity/hydraulic metrics
